Transcrição Completa: 11 - Section 6 (Ideal Customer Template)
[00:00]
"Welcome to section six of the workshop. Okay, you need to download the ideal customer template. It should look like this uh here behind me."
[00:08]
"And what we're going to do is again now we've built out the niche. We understand who you are, what you do, what proof you have, and why people want it."
[00:18]
"Now it's time to map that to what your ideal target and customer is. Okay. So download the template, ideal customer template and let me explain this to you."
[00:29] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Ideal Customer Template"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Template visual exibido ao fundo do apresentador, estrutura de documento com campos para preenchimento de informações sobre cliente ideal
[00:31]
"All right. So this is an example. I want you to use this as the template. But over here we have the target, right?"
[00:38]
"And I just created a name. I made this up. I called it Long Beach Industries, okay? Is the the target company name here. These are really personas for you. So have fun with the name."
[00:52]
"But what this is is what is the industry, right? What is the revenue target you're going after? What is the geography?"
[01:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Target Company Profile"
Industry
Revenue Target
Geography
NÚMEROS: Exemplos mencionados: Fortune 2,000, 5,000, 10,000 companies
VISUAL: Estrutura de campos organizados verticalmente, seção "TARGET" com três categorias principais destacadas
[01:02]
"So, in an example here, this industry could be insurance. Now, you don't have to be one in uh one industry in your niche. You could serve multiple industries, right? That's possible."
[01:14]
"So, you would write out all the different industries, right? Finance, insurance, real estate, um uh orthodontics, whatever it is."
[01:24]
"And then what's the target revenue? This is the company's revenue, right? Are you working with Fortune 2,000 companies, 5,000 companies, 10,000 companies?"
[01:32]
"What's the size of your ideal customer from a revenue perspective? That would go there."
[01:37]
"And then geography, right? Are you a local player? Are you state player? Are you multi-state? Can you service them nationally?"
[01:45]
"You know, you can grow into this, but in your current niche, you know, is there a geography? you know, say like right now we're filming in Florida. Is this Miami, Florida, right?"
[01:56]
"This is what ideal target is. So once you understand that ideal target, this is where most companies stop, but I've taken it a step further."
[02:07]
"I want to map the target to the customer. This is the person actually signing your contract or your PO or your statement of work."
[02:16] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Customer Profile - Billy Bud"
NÚMEROS: Idade: 28 ou 54 (exemplos mencionados), Income: $45,000 vs $250,000 (exemplos comparativos)
VISUAL: Ilustração de persona "Billy Bud" - personagem com mochila e óculos, aparência desajeitada, representação visual do cliente ideal com características específicas
[02:18]
"And this is where I want to get into some of the psychology of this. So, now that we know the target and the customer, in this example, I did Billy Bud."
[02:27]
"Look at Billy Bud. He's a awkward little fella here, isn't he? Billy Bud with his little backpack and glasses."
[02:33]
"But on this is is is your salespeople and you your your company need to understand the actual customer that's, you know, writing the check."
[02:43] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Customer Psychology Profile"
Gender
Age
Communication Preference
Geography
Household Income
Pain Points/Objections
Lifestyle Indicators
NÚMEROS: Exemplos - 54 anos, 28 anos, $45,000 income, $250,000 income, 4x steak dinners per week
VISUAL: Lista estruturada com campos detalhados para análise psicográfica do cliente, organização vertical com categorias comportamentais e demográficas
[02:45]
"So, what's the typical gender of this customer? Does it skew male? Does it skew female? you know, what age is this? You know, who's controlling the budget?"
[02:54]
"Is it somebody that's 54 or is it somebody that's 28?"
[02:58]
"What's the best form of communication that this person likes? You know, do they want a phone call? Do they want an in-person meeting? Do they want email? Do they want text? Do they want a combination of those things?"
[03:10]
"Geography. You know, where are they located? You know, why that's important is, you know, if if you're doing different time zones and there's a certain time you want to catch that person, we need to capture that."
[03:22]
"You know, what's their average household income? You know, what's the typical pain or concern or objections that they're giving you in the sales process, right?"
[03:32]
"If you're not documenting this, you're not getting better. You know, why are you not getting deals? What are you having to overcome? All these things you should write out."
[03:42]
"And then where would they buy clothes and eat dinner at? Okay. When you frame all of this up, you have a really good understanding of who the customer is, how they want to be communicated to, what they're going to respond well to, and what they don't."
[03:57]
"And you know, if this person is on, you know, makes a, you know, 45,000 household income and is eating fast food typically for every meal, how you speak to them is going to be much different than someone that has a 250,000 household income and does, you know, goes out to eat and has steak dinners four times a week."
[04:20]
"Do you see what I'm getting at? When you condition and train your staff to really understand from your niche, which we just defined, who you are, what you do, why you do it, what proof do you have, what your tag is, who you're going after, and now you've mapped that to the target and the buyer."
[04:40]
"Do you see how all these things are getting really, really tight? And when you condition and train against this, when you onboard new staff, whether they're delivery people or they're salespeople, they should have access to this because this sets a precedent on who the customer is, what the expectations are, how they want to be communicated to, what the frequency is, all those things."
[05:04]
"It's super super important. So I want everybody to do, it's workshop time. You should have downloaded the template. If you haven't, please do that now."
[05:14]
"Spend 15, 20 minutes. Okay, you have, you know, it shouldn't take longer than that because one, again, you have the who, what, and why already filled out."
[05:24]
"You have the niche filled out, and you have your competitive analysis. You have all the data you need in order to fill out your ideal target and customer."
[05:33]
"Go fill it out and I'll see you in the next."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Tempo de trabalho sugerido: 15-20 minutos
Exemplos de tamanho de empresa: Fortune 2,000, 5,000, 10,000
Exemplos de renda: $45,000 vs $250,000 household income
Exemplos de idade: 28 anos vs 54 anos
Frequência de jantar: 4x por semana (exemplo lifestyle)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Template de Cliente Ideal dividido em duas seções principais: TARGET e CUSTOMER
Mapeamento de niche para cliente específico
Análise psicográfica detalhada do tomador de decisão
Sistema de documentação de objeções e pain points
Processo de onboarding baseado em perfil de cliente
Metodologia de treinamento de equipe com base em persona
ESTRUTURA:
SEÇÃO TARGET:
Indústria (múltiplas permitidas: finance, insurance, real estate, orthodontics)
Revenue target da empresa
Geografia (local, estadual, multi-state, nacional)
SEÇÃO CUSTOMER (Persona):
Nome da persona (exemplo: Billy Bud)
Gênero típico
Faixa etária
Preferências de comunicação (phone, email, text, in-person)
Geografia/time zone
Household income
Pain points e objeções comuns
Lifestyle indicators (onde compra roupa, onde come)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Household income ranges: $45,000 a $250,000+
Target company revenue: Fortune 2,000 a 10,000 companies
Sem custos mencionados para o template
DURAÇÃO: ~5:38 minutos FORMATO: Workshop/Aula prática com template para download
